name: Sequoia Capital India (Peak XV Partners)
description: India's leading VC fund. Surge program specifically targets early-stage Indian and Southeast Asian startups with $1–$2M checks and an intensive accelerator program.

name: Blume Ventures
description: Early-stage India-focused VC with a strong track record in B2B SaaS and consumer tech. Known for founder-friendly terms and deep local network.

name: Y Combinator
description: World's top startup accelerator. 3-month program in Silicon Valley with intense mentorship, demo day access to 1000+ investors, and a powerful global alumni network. Accepts Indian startups.

name: Indian Angel Network (IAN)
description: India's largest angel network with 500+ investors. Focuses on early-stage Indian startups. Also runs IAN Fund (institutional). Particularly strong in helping first-time founders with mentoring.

name: Nasscom 10,000 Startups
description: India's largest tech startup ecosystem initiative by Nasscom. Provides co-working, mentoring, investor access, go-to-market support, and visibility at flagship events like NASSCOM Product Conclave.

name: Accel India
description: One of India's most active early-stage VCs. Known for backing category-creating companies. Has a dedicated seed program called Accel Atoms for very early stage.

name: Founder Institute India
focus_sectors: all sectors — especially technology, SaaS, marketplace
ticket_size: Equity-free program (small equity pool formed from alumni)
description: World's largest pre-seed startup accelerator with strong India chapters (Bengaluru, Hyderabad, Mumbai). Ideal for idea-stage founders who need structured mentorship to get investor-ready.
